z:TestContains.test_builtin_sequence_types.<locals>.Deviant1z
Behaves strangely when compared
            This class is designed to make sure that the contains code
            works when the list is modified during the check.

test_nonreflexiveE
TestContains.test_nonreflexivec
z4TestContains.test_block_fallback.<locals>.ByContainsc
zATestContains.test_block_fallback.<locals>.ByContains.__contains__Nr
ByContainsT
z7TestContains.test_block_fallback.<locals>.BlockContainsa
Is not a container
            This class is a perfectly good iterable (as tested by
            list(bc)), as well as inheriting from a perfectly good
            container, but __contains__ = None prevents the usual
            fallback to iteration in the container protocol. That
            is, normally, 0 in bc would fall back to the equivalent
            of any(x==0 for x in bc), but here it's blocked from
            doing so.
            c
__iter__c
z@TestContains.test_block_fallback.<locals>.BlockContains.__iter__N)
BlockContainsX
z2TestContains.test_block_fallback.<locals>.<lambda>)
objectZ
assertFalser3
test_block_fallbackR
z TestContains.test_block_fallbackN)
__main__)
collectionsr
unittestZ
test.supportr
TestCaser
mainr
<module>
